examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First-In-First-Out page replacement algorithm to simulate page faults
examples.code:
from collections import deque

def fifo_page_replacement(page_frames, page_sequence):
    frames = deque(maxlen=page_frames)
    page_faults = 0
    
    for page in page_sequence:
        if page not in frames:
            if len(frames) == page_frames:
                frames.popleft()  # Remove oldest
            frames.append(page)
            page_faults += 1
            print(f"Page {page} -> Fault (Frames: {list(frames)})")
        else:
            print(f"Page {page} -> Hit (Frames: {list(frames)})")
    
    return page_faults

# Example: 3 frames, page sequence: 1,2,3,4,1,2,5
num_frames = 3
pages = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5]
faults = fifo_page_replacement(num_frames, pages)
print(f"\nTotal Page Faults: {faults}")
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates FIFO page replacement. It maintains a queue of pages in memory. When a page fault occurs and memory is full, the oldest page (at front of queue) is removed to make room for the new page.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of page references
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) where f is number of frames

examples.title: Contiguous Memory Allocation - First Fit
examples.description: C program demonstrating first-fit contiguous memory allocation
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates contiguous memory allocation using First Fit strategy. It maintains a list of memory blocks and allocates the first available block that is large enough for the requested process size.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of memory blocks

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement algorithm
examples.code:
def lru_page_replacement(page_frames, page_sequence):
    frames = []
    page_faults = 0
    access_order = {}
    time = 0
    
    for page in page_sequence:
        time += 1
        access_order[page] = time
        
        if page not in frames:
            page_faults += 1
            if len(frames) < page_frames:
                frames.append(page)
            else:
                # Find LRU page (least recent access)
                lru_page = min(frames, key=lambda p: access_order.get(p, 0))
                frames.remove(lru_page)
                frames.append(page)
            print(f"Page {page} -> Fault (Frames: {frames})")
        else:
            print(f"Page {page} -> Hit (Frames: {frames})")
    
    return page_faults

# Example: 3 frames, page sequence: 1,2,3,4,1,2,5
num_frames = 3
pages = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5]
faults = lru_page_replacement(num_frames, pages)
print(f"\nTotal Page Faults: {faults}")
examples.explanation: This Python code implements LRU page replacement. It tracks when each page was last accessed using a timestamp dictionary. When a page needs to be replaced, it chooses the page that was used longest ago (smallest access time).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) where n is page references and f is frame count
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) for storing frames and access history
